text.title: Thin Views and Constants Patterns
text.summary: APIView thin-view pattern, TextChoices for enums, constants.py for limits, and the most common antipatterns found in Django PRs.

rule.statement: Views handle three things only: validate input via serializer, call the service, return response. No ORM calls, no business logic, no side effects in views.

example.note: Business logic and side effects in views cannot be tested without the HTTP stack and cannot be reused from management commands or Celery tasks.

rule.statement: Use TextChoices with explicit string values for all model choice fields. Use constants.py for numeric limits and thresholds. Never use magic strings or magic numbers in service code.
rule.rationale: TextChoices with explicit values make enum reordering safe (no DB churn). Constants.py makes thresholds grep-able and reviewable.

section.title: Common Antipatterns

antipattern.description: Bare except: clause on Model.objects.get() calls.
antipattern.reason: Catches every exception including KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit. Always catch the specific exception: Model.DoesNotExist.

antipattern.description: Validation in Model.clean() for DRF API flows.
antipattern.reason: clean() is called by forms, not DRF serializers. Validation there is silently skipped on API requests.

antipattern.description: Serializer with password in Meta.fields without write_only: True.
antipattern.reason: Password hash leaks into API responses. Always add extra_kwargs = {'password': {'write_only': True}}.

section.reference: ruff config with DJ ruleset for automated detection
